title: Stale TODO/FIXME/HACK comments indicate deferred incomplete work
description: TODO, FIXME, HACK, and XXX comments left in production code represent acknowledged technical debt that was never addressed. These markers indicate incomplete implementations, known bugs, or workarounds that may cause failures under edge conditions. Each should be tracked in the pipeline registry (CronAiAthon) and resolved or removed before shipping.
detection_method: Regex scan for TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX comment markers in source code
preventionRule: Do not leave TODO/FIXME/HACK comments in production code. Either implement the fix inline or create a pipeline item (New-PipelineItem -Type 'ToDo') to track it formally, then remove the comment.
remedy: Address the TODO item or register it as a pipeline item with New-PipelineItem, then remove the comment marker. Template files (_TEMPLATE-*.psm1) may retain instructional TODOs.
